items.rationale: Sees restaking as unbundling cryptoeconomic security from the EVM, enabling 100x faster open innovation on consensus, VMs, oracles, and bridges on top of Ethereum.
items.age_note: Thesis set Feb 2024 (led Series B), reaffirmed Apr 2024; ~11-13 months old but core and unreversed.

citations.quote: EigenLayer is the verifiable cloud. Massive leap forward today toward the realization of that vision.
EigenLayer has the potential to unlock 100x faster innovation on technologies like consensus mechanisms, novel virtual machines, decentralized oracles, bridges

items.rationale: Treats fragmentation across rollups/chains as the key barrier to composability; backs shared sequencing (Espresso), messaging (LayerZero), and universal wrapped assets to unify them.
items.age_note: Sustained from Dec 2023 (LayerZero v2) through Feb 2025 (Universal); most recent expression Feb 2025.

citations.quote: building a protocol to make all cryptoassets available on all chains... Think of Universal as a 'Circle for cryptoassets.'
Espresso is building a decentralized network that acts as a coordination mechanism for rollup sequencers.

items.rationale: Believes confidential-but-verifiable computation (ZK, secure hardware for private state) expands the design space into regulated markets like finance, healthcare, and identity.
items.age_note: Recent and accelerating: Aleo Sep 2024, Seismic Mar 2025; most current expression Mar 2025.

citations.quote: Seismic has created a blockchain architecture with a new approach that is based on secure hardware for maintaining private state.
Aleo's breakthrough is to use cutting edge cryptography in the form of zero knowledge proofs to create a blockchain that is privacy-preserving

items.rationale: Holds that decentralizing AI requires verification, and that agents will need to call smart contracts with web2-grade reliability — crypto as the trust layer for AI.
items.age_note: Building view: NEAR May 2024, sharpened Feb-Mar 2025 (verification + agent infra).

citations.quote: How do you decentralize AI? You need verification.
as we approach an internet of agents, Halliday's Workflow Protocol provides the critical infrastructure for AI to call on web3 smart contracts

items.rationale: Argues onchain dev today resembles hardware design (audit, 'tape out', migrate); higher-level primitives/workflow orchestration will let devs iterate at web2 software pace.
items.age_note: Most recent major thesis (Halliday Series A, Mar 2025); freshest signal in corpus.

citations.quote: back their vision for a new 'software era' of web3 development... help web3 graduate from hardware-paced development and accelerate at the pace of software.
Software only ever becomes easier to write via higher level primitives that abstract away the messy low level details. Halliday's launch today does that for smart contracts.

items.rationale: Long-held conviction that Ethereum's endgame is thousands/millions of rollups sharing bedrock security; modularity as 'a constraint that de-constrains' unlocking ecosystem innovation.
items.age_note: Heavily articulated late 2023-early 2024; still foundational to 2025 bets, latest direct mention Mar 2024.

citations.quote: Ethereum's scalability end-game is all about rollups—potentially thousands or even millions of rollups, each one a mini-ecosystem or an application
Exciting news from @NEARProtocol today about their increasing alignment with the modular Ethereum stack

items.rationale: Wants crypto used to scale human cooperation — token lockups, retroactive funding, non-transferable tokens, rewards for verifiable work, iterated games — over short-term extraction.
items.age_note: Stated forcefully Dec 2023; not restated verbatim since, but remains his organizing philosophy reflected in later bets.

citations.quote: Underexplored — token lockups, retroactive funding, non-transferable tokens, rewards for verifiable work, on-chain reputation, iterated games
use it to create long-term alignment of incentives that increase the scale of human cooperation toward building new things.

items.rationale: Explicitly predicts formal verification grows in importance for crypto, consistent with his verifiability-as-trust thesis.
items.age_note: Single dated prediction Dec 2024; recent and unreversed.

citations.quote: 2025 prediction: Formal verification will become increasingly important

items.rationale: Highlights real-world DePIN traction reaching mainstream telecom subscribers as evidence of crypto's utility beyond finance.
items.age_note: Light, single recent signal (Feb 2025); enthusiasm appears more endorsement than core thesis.

citations.quote: Congrats to the @helium team on its @movistarMX partnership, connecting 2.3M subscribers in Mexico

items.rationale: Views meaningless speculation as the primary force holding crypto back, turning a cooperation technology into a zero-sum casino; rejects the 'casino bootstraps the space' argument.
items.age_note: Argued intensely Dec 2023; not explicitly restated since, but no reversal — treat as standing conviction.

citations.quote: We don't need the casino. We never did. It's the primary force holding the space back.
Tired AF — arbitrage, 100x leverage, yield farming, flash loans, MEV capture, memecoins, airdrop farming, day-trading

items.rationale: Frames stagnation/atrophy — driven by 'technically illiterate bureaucrats' and web2 monopolies deciding what can be built — as the real existential risk; urges taking more tech risk.
items.age_note: Strong 2023-Apr 2024; tempered by Feb 2025 optimism on a favorable CFTC nominee, suggesting he sees the US regulatory posture improving.

citations.quote: Our greatest existential risk is the risk of stagnation and atrophy. No civilization can survive very long by standing still.
We need to be taking more risks on new tech, not less.

items.rationale: Worries the rollup-centric roadmap fragments the ecosystem — apps on separate rollups lose seamless composability, hurting UX and slowing innovation; motivates his interop bets.
items.age_note: Articulated Mar 2024; remains live as the problem his interop investments target.

citations.quote: The biggest disadvantage of a rollup-centric future is the fragmentation of the Ethereum ecosystem.
This friction creates capital inefficiency and frustration... it makes it harder for unsophisticated users to enter DeFi and hinders innovation

items.rationale: Cautions that modularity helps only when it adds redundancy, not when separate blockchains for DA/sequencing introduce new single points of failure.
items.age_note: Noted Dec 2023; nuance to his pro-modularity stance, not restated since.

citations.quote: It depends on whether modularity adds redundancy... or adds more single points of failure (as with separate blockchains for DA, etc).

items.rationale: Treats Ethereum as the bedrock of pooled security on which the entire modular/restaking/rollup stack he backs is built.
items.age_note: Consistently central across corpus; restated implicitly through Mar 2025 bets.

citations.quote: build on top of the already decentralized and secure validator set of the most secure programmable blockchain: Ethereum.

items.rationale: Called it the emerging epicenter for AI x crypto and praised its alignment with the modular Ethereum stack.
items.age_note: Most direct praise May 2024 (~10 months old); not restated since, treat as persisted.

citations.quote: NEAR is emerging as the epicenter for AI x crypto.

items.rationale: Cited real-world telecom adoption (Movistar Mexico, 2.3M subscribers) as proof of DePIN traction.
items.age_note: Recent (Feb 2025) but a partnership endorsement rather than an explicit token call.

citations.quote: Congrats to the @helium team on its @movistarMX partnership, connecting 2.3M subscribers in Mexico

items.rationale: Singles out soulless tokens that 'moon on nothing but memes and then fall off a cliff' as emblematic of the casino dynamic he opposes.
items.age_note: Dec 2023; not restated verbatim but consistent with his standing anti-speculation stance.

citations.quote: we don't need tokens without soul or purpose that moon on nothing but memes and then fall off a cliff.

risk_regime.rationale: Yahya is aggressively risk-on toward fundamental technology and building — he argues stagnation, not failure, is the real existential threat and that the space should take MORE tech risk. But this is a quality-filtered, structural risk appetite: he is explicitly risk-off on speculation, leverage, and memecoins. His posture is 'long deep infrastructure (restaking, ZK/privacy, interop, AI-verification, dev abstraction), short the casino.' Feb 2025 optimism on US regulatory leadership reinforces a constructive forward stance.

notes: Yahya's corpus is dominated by investment-announcement theses (EigenLayer, Espresso, Aleo, Universal, Seismic, Halliday) plus a strong recurring philosophy: crypto as a cooperation/verifiability technology, not a casino. Recency weighting pushes ZK/privacy, AI-x-crypto-via-verification, cross-chain assets, and developer abstraction to the top (all Feb-Mar 2025). His anti-speculation and anti-safetyism views are most forcefully stated in Dec 2023 and not restated verbatim later, so they are marked persisted; there is no evidence of reversal, and his 2025 bets remain consistent with them. One directional shift worth flagging: his 2023-2024 hostility toward regulators softens into Feb 2025 optimism about favorable US crypto leadership (CFTC nominee endorsement), implying he sees the regulatory-stagnation risk easing. No statements provide explicit price/market-cycle calls, so the risk regime is inferred from his consistent build-oriented, anti-speculation framing rather than any macro market timing view. Much of the corpus is also non-market personal/fitness content, which carries no signal.
